prompt: Show me the sales in Nov 2021
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('day', s.Sold_Date) AS sale_date,
    SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS daily_sales
FROM
    sales s
WHERE
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2021
    AND EXTRACT(MONTH FROM s.Sold_Date) = 11
GROUP BY
    sale_date
ORDER BY
    sale_date
'''

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

x_data = data_df[config['x_axis']]

if 'y_axes' in config:
    y_axes = config['y_axes']
    n_series = len(y_axes)
    bar_width = 0.8 / n_series
    x = np.arange(len(x_data))

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    for i, col in enumerate(y_axes):
        offset = (i - n_series / 2 + 0.5) * bar_width
        plt.bar(x + offset, data_df[col], width=bar_width, label=col)

    plt.xlabel(config['x_axis'])
    plt.ylabel('Value')
    plt.title(config['title'])
    plt.xticks(x, x_data, rotation=45, ha='right')
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(True, axis='y', alpha=0.3)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

elif 'group_by' in config:
    group_col = config['group_by']
    y_col = config['y_axis']

    groups = sorted(data_df[group_col].unique())
    x_labels = sorted(data_df[config['x_axis']].unique())
    x = np.arange(len(x_labels))
    n_series = len(groups)
    bar_width = 0.8 / n_series

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    for i, group_val in enumerate(groups):
        df_group = data_df[data_df[group_col] == group_val].set_index(config['x_axis'])
        y_vals = [df_group.loc[xl, y_col] if xl in df_group.index else 0 for xl in x_labels]
        offset = (i - n_series / 2 + 0.5) * bar_width
        plt.bar(x + offset, y_vals, width=bar_width, label=str(group_val))

    plt.xlabel(config['x_axis'])
    plt.ylabel(y_col)
    plt.title(config['title'])
    plt.xticks(x, x_labels, rotation=45, ha='right')
    plt.legend()
    plt.grid(True, axis='y', alpha=0.3)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

else:
    y_data = data_df[config['y_axis']]

    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
    plt.plot(x_data, y_data, marker='o')
    plt.xlabel(config['x_axis'])
    plt.ylabel(config['y_axis'])
    plt.title(config['title'])
    plt.xticks(rotation=45, ha='right')
    plt.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

prompt: Return the top 3 brands by realized average selling price in 2023, where average price is computed as total revenue divided by total units sold, along with total units sold per brand
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT
    p.brand,
    SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) / SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS avg_selling_price,
    SUM(s.Qty_Sold) AS total_units_sold
FROM
    sales s
JOIN
    products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded
WHERE
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023
GROUP BY
    p.brand
ORDER BY
    avg_selling_price DESC
LIMIT 3
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 3 rows with columns : brand, avg_selling_price, total_units_sold
Analyzer_1_message: The top 3 brands by realized average selling price in 2023 are CrunchTime ($18.22), GlowUp ($17.93), and CleanHome ($17.11). CrunchTime had the highest average price but sold the fewest units (24,817), while CleanHome had the lowest average price but sold the most units (57,576).

prompt: Show 2023 revenue by region comparing Organic and Non-Organic products as a grouped bar chart
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT
    st.region,
    CASE WHEN pr.is_organic THEN 'Organic' ELSE 'Non-Organic' END AS product_type,
    SUM(sl.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue_2023
FROM
    sales sl
JOIN
    products pr ON sl.SKU_Coded = pr.SKU_Coded AND sl.Product_Class_Code = pr.Product_Class_Code
JOIN
    stores st ON sl.Store_Number = st.Store_Number
WHERE
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sl.Sold_Date) = 2023
GROUP BY
    st.region,
    CASE WHEN pr.is_organic THEN 'Organic' ELSE 'Non-Organic' END
ORDER BY
    st.region,
    product_type
'''

prompt: Show promo revenue share by product category for 2023 as a bar chart, expressing the share as a percentage of total revenue

I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT
    p.category AS product_category,
    SUM(CASE WHEN s.On_Promo = 1 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) AS promo_revenue,
    SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value) AS total_revenue,
    ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN s.On_Promo = 1 THEN s.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(s.Total_Sale_Value), 2) AS promo_revenue_share_pct
FROM
    sales s
JOIN
    products p ON s.SKU_Coded = p.SKU_Coded AND s.Product_Class_Code = p.Product_Class_Code
WHERE
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM s.Sold_Date) = 2023
GROUP BY
    p.category
ORDER BY
    promo_revenue_share_pct DESC
'''

prompt: Return the top 10 region-category combinations by year-over-year revenue growth from 2022 to 2023, showing revenue for both years and the growth expressed as a percentage rounded to 2 decimal places
Retriever_1_message:
I executed this query to retrieve the required data:

'''SQL
SELECT
    st.region,
    pr.category,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sa.Sold_Date) = 2022 THEN sa.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) AS revenue_2022,
    SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sa.Sold_Date) = 2023 THEN sa.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) AS revenue_2023,
    ROUND(
        (SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sa.Sold_Date) = 2023 THEN sa.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END) -
         SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sa.Sold_Date) = 2022 THEN sa.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END)) /
        NULLIF(SUM(CASE WHEN EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sa.Sold_Date) = 2022 THEN sa.Total_Sale_Value ELSE 0 END), 0) * 100,
        2
    ) AS yoy_growth_pct
FROM
    sales sa
JOIN
    products pr ON sa.SKU_Coded = pr.SKU_Coded AND sa.Product_Class_Code = pr.Product_Class_Code
JOIN
    stores st ON sa.Store_Number = st.Store_Number
WHERE
    EXTRACT(YEAR FROM sa.Sold_Date) IN (2022, 2023)
GROUP BY
    st.region, pr.category
ORDER BY
    yoy_growth_pct DESC
LIMIT 10
'''

The resulting DataFrame has 10 rows with columns : region, category, revenue_2022, revenue_2023, yoy_growth_pct
Analyzer_1_message: The top 10 region-category combinations by year-over-year revenue growth from 2022 to 2023 include Midwest Dairy with 61.89% growth (from $109,924.97 to $177,962.09), Northeast Dairy with 52.08% growth (from $97,567.40 to $148,380.87), and West Dairy with 38.41% growth (from $302,663.61 to $418,924.10). The smallest growth in this top 10 was Midwest Personal Care at 0.60% (from $97,222.05 to $97,802.39).
